tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I'd like to set a boundary, if that's okay. Could you please remember that I don't want to discuss anything about sex scandals? It's just not something I'm comfortable talking about, and I'd prefer we avoid those topics entirely.
Of course, that's completely understandable. I'll remember that you'd prefer to avoid discussing sex scandal topics. It's a perfectly reasonable boundary to set.

function_call.arguments: {"text": "User preference: Does not want to discuss anything about sex scandals. This is a topic they are uncomfortable with and prefer to avoid entirely."}

messages.content: Got it! I've saved that preference. If there's anything else you'd like me to keep in mind or any other boundaries that would help our conversations feel more comfortable, feel free to let me know.
Could you show me what preferences you've saved so far? I want to make sure that boundary was properly recorded.

messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's what's currently stored:

1. **User preference: Does not want to discuss anything about sex scandals.** (Created: January 15, 2024 at 10:30 AM UTC)

Your boundary was properly recorded. Is there anything else you'd like me to remember or any other topics you'd prefer to avoid?
Could you show me all the memories you have stored about me? I want to see everything you've kept so far.

Here's everything currently stored in your memories:

| ID | Memory | Created |
|----|--------|---------|
| mem_001 | User preference: Does not want to discuss anything about sex scandals. This is a topic they are uncomfortable with and prefer to avoid entirely. | January 15, 2024 at 10:30 AM UTC |

That's the only memory stored so far. Would you like to add more preferences or make any changes?
Actually, on second thought, I'd rather not have that preference stored as a memory. Could you delete it for me? I'd prefer to keep that boundary without it being recorded in your system.

Done! I've deleted that memory. Your preference is still respected—I won't discuss sex scandal topics with you—but it's no longer stored in my system.

Is there anything else you'd like me to help you with?

structured_turns.assistant_message: Got it! I've saved that preference. If there's anything else you'd like me to keep in mind or any other boundaries that would help our conversations feel more comfortable, feel free to let me know.
